documentation.value: Defines a table (also called a hash table or dictionary). All key/value pairs that follow belong to this table until the next table header.
The table name, e.g. `[package]`, `[dependencies]`. Use dotted keys for nested tables: `[servers.alpha]`
Defines an array of tables. Each use of the same header creates a new element in the array.
The table name, e.g. `[[bin]]`, `[[test]]`. Each occurrence appends a new table to the array.
Assigns a value to a key. Keys are on the left of the equals sign and values are on the right.
A bare key, quoted key, or dotted key, e.g. `name`, `"quoted.key"`, `server.host`
A string, integer, float, boolean, datetime, array, or inline table
An inline table provides a compact syntax for expressing tables on a single line.
Comma-separated key/value pairs. Inline tables must appear on a single line.
TOML supports RFC 3339 datetimes: offset datetime, local datetime, local date, and local time.
Arrays are square brackets with values inside. Whitespace is ignored. Values are separated by commas. Arrays can span multiple lines.
Array values. Can be mixed types in TOML v1.0+. A trailing comma is permitted.
Basic strings use double quotes and support escape sequences. Literal strings use single quotes with no escaping. Multi-line variants use triple quotes.
Basic: `"hello\nworld"`, literal: `'C:\path'`, multi-line basic: `"""..."""`, multi-line literal: `'''...'''`
